summary: Outstanding audience blend with deep, wide stereo imaging captures a solid, typical 1984 Grateful Dead show.
review: Both reviewers praise the audience recording for its remarkable depth, true‑stereo image and minimal post‑mixing, noting that the placement of the taper yields a clear separation of Garcia on the right and Lesh on the left. The performance itself is described as a good, representative 1984 two‑set show, making the release highly recommended despite the lack of specific song highlights.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for recording quality (excellent audience blend) and a solid, typical 1984 performance leads to an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Deep, wide soundstage; two‑mic blend yields true stereo imaging with Garcia on the right and Lesh on the left. Bass presence is heavy but raw, no post‑mixing; praised as comparable to broadcast quality despite using NAKs and Senn 421 microphones.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical energetic 1984 two‑set flow with solid playing throughout; no specific song issues mentioned.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Stereo placement puts Garcia on the right side of the image, noted as clear and distinct.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh is heard prominently on the left side, highlighting his bass presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mix captures both drummers well in true stereo, praised for handling the dual‑drum setup.
